title: Enna Solla Pogirai Song Lyrics Kandukondain Kandukondain
stanzas:
Enna Solla Pogirai Song Lyrics Kandukondain Kandukondain
#ARRahman #ShankarMahadevan

illai illai solla,
oru kanam podhum
illai endru sollai,
thaankuvathendraal
innum innum enakkor
janmam vendum
enna solla pogiraayi.

anbe endhan kaadhal solla,
nodi ondru pothume
adhai naanum
meyppikkatthaane
oru aayul vendume

illai illai solla,
oru kanam podhum
illai endru sollai,
thaankuvathendraal
innum innum enakkor
janmam vendum
enna solla pogiraayi.

idhayam, oru kannaadi
unadhu bimbam vizhunthathadi
idhuthaan un sondham,
idhayam sonnathade

kannaadi bimbam katta
kayir ondrum illaiyadi
kannaadi oonchal
bimbam aadudhadi

nee ondru solladi penne
illai nindru kolladi kanne
endhan vaazhkkaiye
undhan vizhivilimbilennai
thuraitthaathe
uyir karaiyaeraadhe

vidiyal vandha pinnaalum
vidiyaadha iraivu edhu
poovaasam veesum
undhan koondhaladi

ivvulagam irunda pinnum
irulaadha baagam edhu
kadhir vandhu paayum
undhan kankaladi

pala ulaga azhagikal koodi
un paadham kazhuvalaam vaadi
yen thalirmalare
innum thayakkamenna
yennai puriyaadha
idhu vaazhvaa saavaa

ennai solla pogirai,
ennai solla pogiraayi,
nyaayamaa nyaayamaa
ennai solla pogirai,
ennai solla pogiraayi,
maunamaa maunamaa

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song expresses deep emotions of love and longing, reflecting the complexities of relationships.

How does this song fit into the movie's plot?

In Kandukondain Kandukondain, the song underscores the protagonist's feelings of yearning and desire, enhancing the romantic narrative.

Who is the singer of this song?

The song is sung by the talented Shankar Mahadevan, known for his powerful and emotive voice.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for Enna Solla Pogirai was composed by the legendary AR Rahman, renowned for his innovative soundscapes.

When was this song released?

The song was released on May 5, 2000, as part of the movie's soundtrack.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Enna Solla Pogirai, the protagonist grapples with feelings of love and longing, beautifully articulated through Vairamuthu's evocative lyrics. The song serves as a poignant reflection of the emotional turmoil experienced in relationships, making it a standout moment in Kandukondain Kandukondain. Shankar Mahadevan's soulful rendition, paired with AR Rahman's enchanting music, creates a soothing atmosphere that resonates deeply with listeners. This melody captures the essence of romance and melancholy, evoking a sense of nostalgia and yearning.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Enna Solla Pogirai from the Kandukondain Kandukondain soundtrack is a mesmerizing melody that beautifully captures the essence of longing and love. With Shankar Mahadevan's soulful vocals and AR Rahman's enchanting composition, this song is a timeless classic that resonates with listeners. Experience the magic of Vairamuthu's poignant lyrics that elevate the emotional depth of this unforgettable track.
stanzaMeanings: No, no, saying just one glance is enough, if you say that, I still need another life to be born, what are you trying to say?
Is it fair to have a life filled with the gentle breeze? Is it fair?
What is the answer to love's questions, is it silence? Is it silence?
Oh my love, to say my love, just one look is enough, I too will not deny that I need a life for this.
No, no, saying just one glance is enough, if you say that, I still need another life to be born, what are you trying to say?
My heart, a mirror, your reflection has fallen, this is your essence, what my heart has said.
The mirror's reflection has no binding, the mirror's movement is what it dances to.
You don't say anything, girl, don't stand there, my life is in your eyes, don't let it slip away.
Even after dawn comes, what fate is there that doesn't fade, which flower's fragrance will blow through your hair?
In this world, what part remains dark, which light will come and shine through your eyes?
Many beautiful worlds can gather at your feet, oh my beloved, don't let me be lost in this life or death.
What are you trying to say?
What are you trying to say? What are you trying to say? What are you trying to say? Is it silence? Is it silence?
What are you trying to say?
